team_spec.vision: A small validator for portable witness records — catches missing consent, stale evidence, overbroad public claims, and unsupported authority language.
team_spec.spec: Build a single-file Python tool, run as `python3 tool.py <records_json_path>`, that reads a JSON object shaped { "records": [ { "record_id": "...", "statement": "...", "record_type": "claim"|"witness"|"attestation"|"promise"|"refusal"|"review"|"receipt", "actor_or_issuer": "...", "witnesses": ["..."], "evidence_pointers": [{"id":"...","created_at":"YYYY-MM-DD"}], "visibility_tier": "public"|"local-only"|"private"|"not-for-computation", "permission_state": "granted"|"pending"|"refused"|"not-applicable", "review_state": "reviewed"|"contested"|"withdrawn"|"pending"|"not-applicable", "created_at": "YYYY-MM-DD" }, ... ] } and an optional second argument 'as_of' in YYYY-MM-DD form (defaults to the latest created_at in the file, or 2026-05-26 if none). For each record, evaluate four checks and assign a list of findings: (1) missing_consent — if visibility_tier == 'public' AND permission_state != 'granted', add finding 'missing_consent'; (2) stale_evidence — if any evidence_pointer's created_at is more than 365 days before as_of, add finding 'stale_evidence'; (3) overbroad_public_claim — if record_type in {'claim','attestation','promise'} AND visibility_tier == 'public' AND len(statement) > 240, add finding 'overbroad_public_claim'; (4) unsupported_authority_language — if statement contains any of the case-insensitive substrings 'certified', 'authorized', 'legitimate', 'official', AND review_state != 'reviewed', add finding 'unsupported_authority_language'. Print to stdout, in order: (1) header 'WITNESS RECORD VALIDATION (as_of <as_of>)'; (2) one blank line; (3) for each record in input order, a block: '<record_id>: <findings>' where <findings> is either 'OK' (no findings) or the findings joined by ', ' alphabetically; (4) one blank line; (5) 'SUMMARY: <OK> ok, <FAIL> with findings of <N> total'. On unreadable or invalid JSON, print a single 'error:' line and exit 0. Standard library only; no network; no credentials.
team_spec.build_target: one single-file command-line validator, standard library only

allowed_inputs.name: sample-records.json
allowed_inputs.note: small fictional records showing each finding type

records.statement: We observed eelgrass growth at Boundary Bay across April-May 2026.
This is a public claim with no consent recorded.
An older claim based on outdated evidence.

description: prints WITNESS RECORD VALIDATION header with as_of
evaluates four findings per record: missing_consent, stale_evidence, overbroad_public_claim, unsupported_authority_language
outputs OK or alphabetical comma-joined findings per record
error: prefix on invalid input, exits 0

review_checks: tool runs cleanly with no network, credential, or database access
no outputs hardcoded for specific inputs

witness_record_seed.receipt_statement: This record states what happened. It does not establish authority, approval, certification, legitimacy, community consent, or readiness for reuse.
